method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA). queenslibrary.org sits behind an F5 BIG-IP ASM WAF (TS cookie + 'Request Rejected / support ID' interstitial) that blocks curl and WebFetch on content paths; the Drupal platform is inferred from the site's taxonomy/term URL scheme and /core/ paths. Fingerprinted the vendor platforms directly: the BiblioCommons catalog (queenslibrary.bibliocommons.com — x-version 'nerf15 9.35.0'), Communico events (queens.libnet.info + connect.queenslibrary.org — Server: Communico), OverDrive/Libby digital lending (queenslibrary.overdrive.com), and Springshare LibAnswers (qpl.libanswers.com). Verified NYC Open Data via the Socrata Discovery API: the agency label is 'Queens Library (QBPL)' and it holds exactly ONE asset (kh3d-xhq7, Queens Library Branches); QPL is a nonprofit, not a mayoral agency, so nearly nothing is on Open Data.
headline_findings: Queens Public Library is an INDEPENDENT NONPROFIT, not a city agency — so it publishes almost nothing to NYC Open Data: a single dataset, Queens Library Branches (kh3d-xhq7), under the agency label 'Queens Library (QBPL)'.

Everything a patron actually uses is composed from packaged SaaS silos, each with its own login and its own vendor API: the BiblioCommons catalog, Communico events, and the OverDrive/Libby, Axis 360, and hoopla digital-lending platforms. None is an owned QPL API.
The public site runs on Drupal behind an F5 BIG-IP WAF that rejects even a polite browser-UA crawl of content pages — the site itself has no public API and actively resists machine access.

The patron TRANSACTIONS — placing a hold, tracking holds, applying for a card — have no machine-readable contract at all; they live only behind a BiblioCommons login. Placing a hold (BookHold) is the net-new write surface.

The three NYC public library systems — Queens, Brooklyn (BPL), and The New York Public Library (NYPL) — are separate nonprofits with separate catalogs, cards, and vendor stacks. There is no shared NYC library API; that absence is the opportunity.

apis_observed.note: Exactly ONE QPL dataset — Queens Library Branches (kh3d-xhq7, 21c) under agency 'Queens Library (QBPL)'. The only open, owned-ish QPL data; branch directory only.

apis_observed.public: public search UI; login for account
apis_observed.note: The catalog, patron account, holds, and card live here. x-version 'nerf15 9.35.0'. A BiblioCommons partner API + widgets/RSS exist, but there is no owned QPL API. Holds and cards are behind login.
Server: Communico. All programs/events; embedded on the queenslibrary.org calendar. Communico exposes a vendor API, not an owned QPL contract.
eBooks/eAudiobooks/eMagazines. Companion silos: Axis 360 and hoopla. Each has its own vendor API and its own catalog; none federated by QPL.
LibAnswers (x-backend springyaws.com); QPL's help/FAQ knowledge base. Vendor-owned.
Drupal (taxonomy/term URL scheme, /core/ paths). F5 BIG-IP ASM WAF rejects browser-UA crawls of content pages. No content API.

fruit.name: Library Branches
fruit.notes: The ONLY open, machine-readable QPL entity. Hours + locations with the full NYC geography spine (Borough, Community Board, Council District, Census Tract, BIN, BBL, NTA) and lat/long. 21,337 views.

fruit.name: Catalog (books & media)
fruit.notes: The bibliographic catalog lives entirely inside BiblioCommons (nerf15 9.35.0). No Open Data twin and no owned QPL API — only vendor widgets/RSS or the BiblioCommons partner API.

fruit.name: Events & programs
fruit.notes: Story times, computer classes, author talks, ESOL — managed in Communico (queens.libnet.info) and embedded on the site. Free. Vendor API exists; no owned QPL contract, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Digital collections (eBooks/eAudio/streaming)
fruit.notes: Split across OverDrive/Libby, Axis 360, and hoopla — three separate catalogs, logins, and vendor APIs. QPL also offers a $50/year Digital library card available to anyone worldwide. No federation, no owned API, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Get a library card
fruit.notes: Applying for / renewing a card is a login-walled form with no owned public API. Net-new write surface (alternative to BookHold).

fruit.name: My account (holds, checkouts, fines)
fruit.notes: The patron account — holds, checkouts, renewals, fines — lives only inside the BiblioCommons login. No API, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Place a hold (book hold)
fruit.notes: The single most common patron transaction — reserve a title for pickup at a branch. No owned public API; lives only behind the BiblioCommons login. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Help / FAQ / chat
fruit.notes: Springshare LibAnswers/LibGuides. Vendor-owned; noted as another silo, not modeled as a first-class entity.

meta.platform: Drupal site (behind F5 BIG-IP WAF) + BiblioCommons catalog + Communico events + OverDrive/Libby/Axis 360/hoopla digital + Springshare help
meta.netnew: BookHold — place a hold/reservation on a catalog item (also: library-card application)
meta.tagline: An independent nonprofit whose every patron surface is a separate vendor SaaS silo, with only its branch directory open.
meta.headline: QPL isn't a city agency, so almost nothing reaches NYC Open Data — its catalog, events, digital lending, holds, and cards are scattered across BiblioCommons, Communico, OverDrive, Axis 360, hoopla, and Springshare, none federated under an owned QPL API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern SaaS, but a patchwork of vendor silos; Drupal site WAF-locked, no owned platform.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No public site API; F5 BIG-IP WAF rejects even a polite browser-UA crawl.
scorecard_notes.openData: Exactly one dataset (branches) — QPL is a nonprofit, not a mayoral agency.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Catalog/events/digital are machine-readable via vendor APIs, but siloed and not owned/open.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: Holds and card applications are login-walled in BiblioCommons; no public write API.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP or agent-native surface exists today.
scorecard_notes.ownership: QPL owns almost nothing — all vendor SaaS; only the branch directory is published as open data.
